reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that given a family {T_α} of topologies on X, there exists a unique smallest topology on X containing all the T_α. The Lean signature formalizes this as ∃! T' such that: (a) T' is a topology, (b) T' ⊇ each T i (i.e., contains every open set of each T_α — "contains all the collections"), and (c) T' is contained in every other topology T'' satisfying (a) and (b) (the "smallest" property). Clauses (a)+(b)+(c) correctly encode "smallest topology containing all T_α", and the uniqueness in ∃! matches "unique" (which is in any case automatic from the minimality condition). The is_topology definition (contains univ, closed under binary intersection and arbitrary union) is the standard one; ∅ arises as sUnion ∅. The family is indexed by an arbitrary type I, matching "family". No hypotheses are silently added or dropped, and the quantifier structure matches.
